authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: CCR T14 §5.85(a)(1) — Trout, General Statewide Regulations (inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and 10 trout possession limit.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.quotedText: made our way to Richardson Lake for lunch at about 1045. The lake is outside of the DW and is accessible by 4×4’s and ATV’s.

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Lake layer record for gaz_id 265410, "Richardson Lake", Lake, El Dorado County, CA
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":265410,"gaz_name":"Richardson Lake","gaz_featureclass":"Lake","fcode":39009,"state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"El Dorado"}; geometry (Web Mercator -13381966.2326, 4724946.3778000027) converts to lon -120.212248, lat 39.022858, which is ~24m from the file's recorded coordinate (-120.21201, 39.02302) — within tolerance, coordinate confirmed.

parameters.note: Richardson Lake is a shallow lake (informally 'Richardson Reservoir') just outside the west edge of the Lake Tahoe basin near Barker Pass (El Dorado Co.), on the Tahoe Rim Trail / McKinney-Rubicon 4x4 route and outside the Desolation Wilderness. Its outflow drains west toward the upper Miller Creek / Rubicon River (Middle Fork American River) side of the crest — like the sibling Miller Creek and Rubicon River, it is not a Lake Tahoe tributary, so the §7.50(b)(152) Tahoe tributary closure (which reaches flowing tributaries, not standing lakes) does not apply. As a standing lake not listed by name in §7.40(b) or §7.50(b), the general statewide lake regulation §5.85(a)(1) governs — open all year, 5/10 — the same treatment as other unlisted Sierra lakes (e.g., Lake Schmidell, Lake Genevieve).

regulations.humanSummary: Open all year (general statewide lake regulation; not listed as a special-regulation water)
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.
